title: Tangy Low Cal Korean Squash Stew
description: A peppery, crisp Korean stew with tender zucchini and bell pepper, brightened by gochujang and rice vinegar for a tangy twist, served straight from the cazuela.
instructions: Slice the zucchini and bell pepper into bite‑sized cubes.
Dice the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat the sesame oil in a cazuela over medium heat and sauté the onion and garlic until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the zucchini, bell pepper, and a splash of water; stir to combine.
Mix the gochujang, soy sauce, and rice vinegar in a small bowl, then pour the mixture into the pot.
Season with black pepper, salt, and a pinch of dried seaweed if using; bring the stew to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in chopped scallions at the last minute, then adjust seasoning with extra salt or pepper if needed.
Serve hot straight from the cazuela and enjoy.
